39021_SC21ATP _70.0_34888_ARVIN _70.0_.rejection_reason: CAISO explicitly deprioritized: 'Congestion on radial transmission lines or some local areas may not be selected as a high-priority study even though the congestion cost or duration are relatively large and if the congestion was only driven by local renewable generators identified in the CPUC default renewable portfolio.' TPP identified 70 kV Kern constraints (Copus-Old River, South Kern Jct-San Emidio) but concluded 'no policy-driven upgrades identified' — SSN (special study needed) only.
39021_SC21ATP _70.0_34888_ARVIN _70.0_.notes: #1 most congested line. CAISO is AWARE of 70 kV Kern congestion but explicitly defers action — treats it as potentially transient because congestion may shift if CPUC changes which solar projects get mapped to which substations in the next IRP cycle. 30% of binding hours are Base Case (direct thermal overload), not just contingency. Not in PG&E SB1006 DLR or reconductoring candidates. 2,152 hours of actual market binding over 12 months contradicts 'modeling artifact' framing.

31336_HPLND JT_60.0_31370_CLVRDLJT_60.0_.notes: Near Eagle Rock-Fulton-Silverado 115 kV reconductor ($92.9M, approved) and Konocti-Eagle Rock 60 kV reconductor ($32.5M, approved). Also identified as DLR candidate in PG&E SB1006 Table 1 (historical congestion).

24723_CONTROL _115_24865_TAP188 _115_BR.notes: GENUINELY OVERLOOKED. #3 most congested line, 4,071 hours binding, 100% Base Case (direct thermal overload). SCE Eastern area shows only $9.63M in TPP 2039 model, driven by data center load growth at Valley 115 kV bus. SCE's SB1006 reconductoring proposals focused on Pardee-Santa Clara and Santa Clara-Vincent 230 kV in the LA basin — not the eastern desert. Julian Hinds-Mirage 230 kV advanced reconductor approved ($76M) but is a different line in the same general area. This 115 kV line appears to have no proposed intervention from any source.

22208_EL CAJON_69.0_22408_LOSCOCHS_69.0_.notes: GENUINELY OVERLOOKED. #4 most congested, binding 4,090 hours/year — nearly half of all hours. Appears prominently in DMM monthly reports (DMM Table 5.3 rank #18 by DLAP price impact). SDG&E TPP area shows $12.34M in 230 kV congestion, but this 69 kV sub-transmission congestion is a distinct problem likely driven by local load pockets and rooftop solar backfeed. TPP SDG&E section focuses exclusively on 230 kV and 500 kV upgrades (Sycamore-Scripps, Downtown Reliability Reinforcement $500M, Coronado $108M). The 69 kV sub-transmission layer is structurally deprioritized by the planning process. SDG&E's SB1006 report was not available for this analysis.

30750_MOSSLD _230_30797_LASAGUIL_230_BR.rejection_reason: All Path 15 new-build alternatives rejected in TPP. DLR proposed in PG&E SB1006 but not yet approved by CAISO.
30750_MOSSLD _230_30797_LASAGUIL_230_BR.notes: STUDIED BUT DEFERRED. #5 most congested, 81% solar hours. Path 15 corridor ($521M/yr modeled congestion by 2039 — largest single area). CAISO studied 10 alternatives in TPP 2024-25 Table 4.8-1, all involving major new 500 kV construction or HVDC — none approved ('ratepayer benefit insufficient'). CAISO is locked in multi-year analysis of billion-dollar 500 kV alternatives while this 230 kV line keeps binding every day. PG&E SB1006 confirmed as DLR candidate (historical congestion). DLR cost: $50K-$500K per line vs. 500 kV new-build at $1B+.

30765_LOSBANOS_230_30790_PANOCHE _230_BR.rejection_reason: Ratepayer benefit insufficient to cover costs. TPP Alternative 10: 10 ohm series reactor on each Panoche-Gates 230 kV studied and rejected.
30765_LOSBANOS_230_30790_PANOCHE _230_BR.notes: STUDIED BUT DEFERRED. #6 most congested, 86% solar hours. Part of Path 15 corridor. Series reactor on Panoche-Gates 230 kV studied as TPP Alternative 10 — rejected ('ratepayer benefit insufficient'). CAISO's off-peak deliverability assessment explicitly flagged Gates-Panoche 230 kV with 'reconductor if economic' as potential mitigation — meaning CAISO's own planners have considered reconductoring this exact corridor but haven't yet run the cost-benefit. All 10 new-build 500 kV alternatives also rejected.

30790_PANOCHE _230_30900_GATES _230_BR.rejection_reason: TPP series reactor rejected (ratepayer benefit insufficient). DLR identified as candidate in PG&E SB1006 Table 1 but not yet approved.
30790_PANOCHE _230_30900_GATES _230_BR.notes: STUDIED BUT DEFERRED. #7 most congested, 87% solar hours. Same Path 15 corridor. TPP series reactor (Alt 10) specifically for this line — rejected. PG&E SB1006 identifies as DLR candidate for both historical and future congestion scenarios. CAISO off-peak assessment flagged 'reconductor if economic.' CAISO is struggling with the scale of investment needed for Path 15 while incremental GETs/reconductoring solutions on the 230 kV feeder lines remain unapproved.

34548_KETTLEMN_70.0_34552_GATES _70.0_.rejection_reason: Same as SC21A Tap-Arvin: CAISO explicitly deprioritizes 70 kV Kern sub-transmission congestion. TPP states congestion on radial lines 'may not be selected as a high-priority study' when 'driven by local renewable generators identified in the CPUC default renewable portfolio.' Treated as potentially transient modeling artifact.
34548_KETTLEMN_70.0_34552_GATES _70.0_.notes: EXPLICITLY DEFERRED. #8 most congested, 91% solar hours — strongest solar correlation of any top constraint. 100% Base Case binding (direct thermal overload, no contingency). 70 kV in Gates area, same sub-transmission network as #1 SC21A Tap-Arvin. CAISO is aware of 70 kV Kern congestion but explicitly defers: treats it as potential modeling artifact that may shift with IRP portfolio changes. 1,084 hours of actual market binding suggests otherwise.

34116_LE GRAND_115_34115_ADRA TAP_115_BR.notes: FLAGGED FOR GETs, NOT RECONDUCTORING. #9 most congested, 71% solar hours. PG&E Fresno 115 kV area — TPP attributes congestion to 'load serving or loop flow between 115 kV and 230 kV system' with only $4.55M modeled cost. PG&E SB1006 identified this exact line as a DLR candidate (Table 1, historical congestion). Flagged for GETs but not for reconductoring. Near approved GWF-Kingsburg 115 kV reconductor ($81.6M, in-service 2029).

24723_CONTROL _115_24866_TAP189 _115_BR.notes: GENUINELY OVERLOOKED. #10 most congested, same Control-Inyokern corridor as #3 (Control-Tap188). 100% Base Case (direct thermal overload). Very high avg shadow price ($249/MWh). SCE's SB1006 focused reconductoring proposals on LA basin 230 kV lines, not eastern desert 115 kV. This corridor has two of the top 10 most congested elements with no proposed intervention.

34214_LOS BANS_70.0_30765_LOSBANOS_230_X.notes: Los Banos 70/230 kV transformer. Cannot be reconductored. Part of the Path 15 corridor ($521M/yr by 2039). 80% solar hours. The transformer constraint reflects the broader Path 15 bottleneck at the Los Banos hub.

30765_LOSBANOS_230_30766_PADR FLT_230_BR.notes: 80% solar hours. Part of broader Path 15 congestion. No specific intervention.

33020_MORAGA _115_35101_SN LNDRO_115_BR.notes: South Oakland Reinforcement Project ($250M) approved. Reconductor Moraga-San Leandro #1,2,3 to 2,288 Amps minimum. PG&E to evaluate advanced conductors. In-service May 2032.

34418_KINGSBRG_115_34428_CONTADNA_115_BR.notes: Near GWF-Kingsburg 115 kV reconductor ($81.6M, approved, in-service 2029). May be directly addressed by this project.

24602_VICTOR _115_24607_ROADWAY _115_BR.notes: Lugo-Victor 230 kV #1-4 reconductor previously approved (in-service 2032). 80% solar hours. 115 kV feeder from Victor substation.

24791_TAP710 _115_24731_INYOKERN_115_BR.notes: 115 kV in Kern/Inyo County. Julian Hinds-Mirage 230 kV reconductor approved in nearby area but different line.

35618_SN JSE A_115_35620_EL PATIO_115_BR.notes: South Bay Reinforcement Project ($434M) approved. El Patio-San Jose A 115 kV explicitly listed for reconductoring to 3,000 Amps. In-service May 2032.

32214_RIO OSO _115_32225_BRNSWKT1_115_BR.notes: 115 kV in Sierra/North Valley area. No specific intervention proposed.

31334_CLER LKE_60.0_31338_KONOCTI6_60.0_.notes: 60 kV near Konocti. Konocti-Eagle Rock 60 kV reconductor approved ($32.5M). Also identified as DLR candidate in PG&E SB1006 Table 1 (historical congestion).

34101_CERTANJ2_115_34116_LE GRAND_115_BR.notes: 115 kV in Fresno area. TPP flagged Wilson-Le Grand 115 kV for reconductoring if economic.

34418_KINGSBRG_115_34405_FRWT TAP_115_BR.notes: Kingsburg-Fruit Tap 115 kV. Identified as DLR candidate in PG&E SB1006 Table 1 (historical congestion). Near approved GWF-Kingsburg reconductor ($81.6M).

33916_CURTISS _115_33917_FBERBORD_115_BR.notes: Curtiss-Fiberboard 115 kV. Identified as DLR candidate in PG&E SB1006 Table 1 (historical congestion).

33936_MELNS JB_115_33951_VLYHMTP1_115_BR.notes: Melones JB-Valley Home Tap 115 kV. Identified as DLR candidate in PG&E SB1006 Table 1 (historical congestion).

34115_ADRA TAP_115_34154_DAIRYLND_115_BR.notes: Adra Tap-Dairyland 115 kV. Identified as DLR candidate in PG&E SB1006 Table 1 (historical congestion).

31220_EGLE RCK_115_31228_HOMSTKTP_115_BR.notes: Eagle Rock-Homestake Tap 115 kV. Identified as DLR candidate in PG&E SB1006 Table 1 (historical congestion). Near approved Eagle Rock-Fulton-Silverado reconductor ($92.9M).
